tradeoff_analysis: Sentence-level chunking preserves complete atomic facts, so each retrieved chunk maps cleanly to a single answer point — ideal for narrow fact-lookup queries. Fixed-size chunking may split a sentence across two chunks, degrading TF-IDF signal and potentially returning a partial fact. However, fixed-size chunks can capture more context per chunk for longer, multi-sentence questions, improving recall at the expense of precision. For this knowledge base (short, assertion-dense articles) sentence chunking typically wins on top-3 hit rate.
